CREATE TABLE test_values (a FLOAT, b FLOAT);
SELECT sql_max(2.5, sql_max(3.1, 3.8)) AS max_value FROM test_values;
SELECT user_id, sql_max(order_value, refund_value) AS max_transaction FROM orders GROUP BY user_id;
CREATE TABLE orders (user_id INT, order_value FLOAT, refund_value FLOAT);
SELECT sql_max(o1.order_value, (SELECT sql_max(order_value) FROM orders WHERE user_id = o1.user_id)) FROM orders o1;
SELECT sql_max(a, (SELECT sql_max(b) FROM test_values WHERE a IS NOT NULL)) FROM test_values AS t1;
SELECT sql_max(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders, test_values WHERE orders.user_id = test_values.a) AS max_count FROM test_values GROUP BY b;
